findings.title: toSortedVideos lacks direct test coverage; integration tests don't assert sort order
findings.why_it_matters: toSortedVideos() is a new private helper that determines import ordering. Integration tests in courseImport.test.ts call the full import pipeline but only assert videoCount, not that video[0] has the correct order or filename. A sort regression in toSortedVideos wouldn't be caught by existing tests. Since the function is module-private, direct unit tests aren't possible without exporting it — but adding sort-order assertions to existing integration tests (checking that the first video is the path-sorted first) would close this gap.
evidence: courseImport.test.ts tests importCourseFromFolder but only asserts videoCount (line 103), not video order
A sort regression (e.g., removing the .sort() call) would pass all existing tests
findings.suggested_fix: Add order assertion to existing integration test (e.g., after importing a course with videos at paths '01-intro/1.mp4' and '02-advanced/1.mp4', assert that the first video has order:1 and filename matching the path-sorted first entry). No need to export toSortedVideos.

testing_gaps: Integration tests don't assert on video sort order after import
